mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is particularly useful for applications requiring semantic search and matching. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient resources to handle high loads and the complexity of managing configurations for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the Qdrant CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Qdrant instances.

```bash

curl -sSfL https://get.qdrant.tech/cli.sh | sh

```

> This command downloads and installs the Qdrant CLI.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Qdrant cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

qdrant cluster create --version 1.13 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created Qdrant cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/kubeconfig.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the kubeconfig file from the cluster creation step.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Qdrant cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Qdrant cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This configures autoscaling for your Qdrant deployment.
> This sets up monitoring for your Qdrant deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

qdrant cluster delete --name <your-cluster-name>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-name> with the actual name of your cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

```

> This command deletes all persistent volume claims.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step includes draining nodes, upgrading, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

qdrant cluster upgrade --version 1.13

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> This command upgrades your Qdrant cluster to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to uncordon after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant server may not be running or is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods with 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Qdrant pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resource limits in your cluster configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database connection timeout'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may be overloaded or misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Review the Qdrant configuration and logs for any errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid API key'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The API key used to access Qdrant is incorrect or missing.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct API key is set in your Qdrant configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for advanced filtering options and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration format; users must migrate to the new format.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configuration files to match the new schema outlined in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in a 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
